summary: iter296 backend regression + new-feature testing for ZK-all-models support. Ran full biometric suite: 17 pre-existing tests (test_biometric_zk_all_models.py 5, test_biometric_auto_sync.py 9, test_biometric_iter295_extras.py 3) PLUS a new 2-test regression file exercising DELETE-cancels-pending-jobs and /models catalog through the PUBLIC URL. All 19/19 passed.
critical_code_review_comments: biometric_routes.py is ~1186 lines — approaching the 700-line threshold; consider splitting into device/queue/models submodules.
delete_biometric_device performs a hard delete (delete_one) despite the docstring saying 'soft-disable' — either update docstring or convert to soft-delete for auditability.
test_connection now enqueues a probe job (good), but there's no automatic timeout for probe jobs left in 'pending' if agent is offline — consider a TTL/watchdog to mark stale probe jobs as failed.
seed_data_creation: New test creates a device 'ZK296-DEL-<ts>' in branch 76f56acc-6948-4a2f-bbf4-feccbddea88f, immediately deleted by the same test (cleanup implicit).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter296 confirmed: (1) all 5 new zk-all-models tests pass, (2) all 12 prior tests still pass, (3) DELETE cancels pending jobs (verified via public URL — cancelled_jobs >= 1 after enqueuing a probe), (4) /biometric/devices/models returns full protocols/device_types/supported_models. Frontend not tested per request. Note the login response key is 'token' (not 'access_token').
